name: Sep-dinner: Oxtail Consomm
recipeInstructions:
1. Make the consommé the day before and chill overnight to allow the fat to congeal on top.
2. Gently scoop the hardened fat off the surface using a slotted spoon.
3. In a large 24-cup stockpot, heat oil over medium-high heat; cook oxtails for about 5 minutes or until browned. Remove from pot and set aside.
4. Add onion, leek, celery, carrot, and garlic to the same pot and cook for 5 minutes or until softened.
5. Stir in beef stock, water (4 cups), oxtails, bay leaves, cloves, tomato paste, peppercorns, nutmeg, and thyme. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat and simmer, covered, for 2 hours.
6. Using a fine sieve, strain the consommé into a clean container. Refrigerate uncovered for about 12 hours or until fat has congealed.
7. Remove and discard the congealed fat using a slotted spoon.
8. Pour the strained consommé back into a clean stockpot.
9. Whisk egg whites until foamy, then stir in cold consommé.
10. Over medium-high heat, continue stirring the egg mixture until it reaches 98°F (37°C) or body temperature.
11. Reduce heat to low and make a half-inch wide hole in the egg white using the handle of a wooden spoon.
12. Simmer gently for 15-20 minutes or until the consommé is clear.
13. Using a cheesecloth-lined sieve, ladle the consommé into serving dishes, discarding the egg whites.
14. Season with salt and pepper to taste.
